serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker serving of soya sauce chicken dry noodles is approximately 350 g, comprising a portion of thin wheat noodles tossed in soy sauce and chilli sauce, topped with sliced chicken and chye sim.

nutrient_source.title: Soya sauce chicken noodles (dry) — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Soya sauce chicken noodles (dry)', cr_id F05060122. All core macros and secondary nutrients sourced from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — wheat noodles (boiled)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for soya sauce chicken dry noodles as a composite dish. GI of 52 is estimated from boiled wheat noodles (GI ~52, University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008), which form the dominant carbohydrate component. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 12.17 × 3.5) / 100 ≈ 22.3 for a 350 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 210 mg/100 g; a 350 g serving delivers approximately 735 mg sodium, representing about 32% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and avoid adding extra condiments at the table, as a full serving can contribute up to one-third of the recommended daily sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat noodles with low dietary fibre (0.69 g/100 g) and a glycemic load of approximately 22 per serving elevate postprandial glucose risk.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, request additional non-starchy vegetables such as chye sim or bean sprouts to increase fibre, and consider wholegrain or lower-GI noodle alternatives where available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 210 mg/100 g and estimated saturated fat at 1.5 g/100 g contribute to cardiovascular risk when consumed in full hawker-sized portions.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate portion size, request less soy sauce to reduce sodium load, and choose lean chicken breast over skin-on cuts to limit saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Soya sauce chicken dry noodles (豉油鸡捞面) is a popular Singaporean hawker dish consisting of thin boiled wheat noodles tossed dry — without soup — in a savoury dressing of soy sauce and chilli sauce, topped with sliced poached or braised chicken and blanched chye sim (Chinese flowering cabbage). The noodles are typically springy and lightly coated in the dark, umami-rich soy-based sauce. The dish is served at room temperature or slightly warm and is a common breakfast or lunch option at hawker centres and coffee shops across Singapore. Nutritionally, a 100 g portion provides 137 kcal, 7.93 g protein, 5.94 g fat, and 12.17 g carbohydrate, with 210 mg sodium and only 0.69 g dietary fibre, reflecting the refined wheat noodle base. The moderate sodium content and low fibre make portion control important for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The dish is halal-certified in most hawker settings and is dairy-free, though it contains gluten and soy allergens. It is culturally associated with Singaporean Chinese hawker cuisine and is commonly consumed as a working lunch or quick meal.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05060122) at confidence 0.95. This is an exact match from the primary approved regional source for Singaporean dishes. GI is estimated from the closest approved analogous source (University of Sydney GI Database, boiled wheat noodles) and is clearly documented.
notes: Saturated fat estimated at 1.5 g/100 g based on typical composition of chicken and soy-sauce-dressed wheat noodle dishes from HPB and USDA SR Legacy data; the HPB source does not report saturated fat separately for this entry. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this dish and no sufficiently close approved-source analogue provides reliable per-100 g estimates for the composite preparation. Input cultural tags 'western' and 'plant_based' were corrected: this is a Singaporean Chinese hawker dish containing chicken and is not plant-based. Dietary tag 'low_calorie' was retained as informational only; at 137 kcal/100 g the dish is moderate in energy density. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate from boiled wheat noodles per University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this composite dish was found in approved sources.
